company.name: Gilead Sciences
company.subtitle: HIV $20.8B · Lenacapavir franchise · Trodelvy 1L TNBC · No LOE until 2036
company.cashRunway: Cash generative ($29.4B revenue, profitable, 3.2% div yield)

market.notes: PURPOSE 1+2 positive — twice-yearly injection for PrEP. Game-changer for prevention. $150M first year. Peak $5-10B franchise potential. BIC/LEN combo tablet H2 2026 for treatment simplification.

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~1.2M US PrEP-eligible (MSM, high-risk hetero, PWID); ~400K currently on PrEP (Truvada/Descovy/Apretude).

eu.patientsK.note: ECDC: ~600K EU-5 PrEP-eligible; uptake highest France/UK/Germany; oral generic TDF/FTC dominates.

row.patientsK.note: WHO global PrEP-eligible ~15M; ex-US/EU ~8M accessible (SSA focus via PEPFAR/Global Fund).

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- ACA preventive services $0 copay mandate; long-acting cabotegravir (Apretude) Q2M adherence advantage.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- national HIV programs cover; generic TDF/FTC dominant; LA injectable slower uptake.

row.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- PEPFAR/Global Fund drive access; lenacapavir 6-monthly Q2025+ game-changer in SSA.

us.priceK.note: Apretude ~$22K/yr (Q2M IM); Descovy ~$24K/yr oral daily; generic TDF/FTC <$0.5K/yr.

eu.priceK.note: Apretude ~$15K/yr post-HTA; oral generic TDF/FTC ~$0.3K/yr dominates EU prescriptions.

row.priceK.note: Generic oral <$0.1K/yr via PEPFAR; LA cabotegravir Gilead tiered $0-5K; lenacapavir access pricing pending.

penPct.note: ~35% peak PrEP uptake within eligible pool; long-acting formulations driving growth from ~33% current.

us.reachPct.note: Yeztugo lenacapavir PrEP twice-yearly; game-changer but early ramp

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 55% -- established payor adoption + formulary coverage

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 4% of 600K patients; lower than US due to HTA gating + biosimilar pressure

eu.priceK.note: EU net $18K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 1% of 8000K -- Japan + select markets; limited ex-Japan access

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 8% -- Japan PMDA reimbursed; emerging markets patchy

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $6K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: ASCENT-04/KEYNOTE-D19: Trodelvy + Keytruda 35% reduced progression risk in 1L mTNBC. $1.4B 2025 (+6%). 1L TNBC sNDA → PDUFA H2 2026. If approved: doubles Trodelvy addressable market.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: TNBC ~15% of breast = ~45K incident/yr, ~60K prevalent on-tx (short OS limits prevalence). Source: NCI SEER, TNBC Foundation.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 TNBC ~52K incident; ~65K prevalent. EUROCARE + ESMO TNBC consortium.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~120K on-tx; TNBC over-represented in AA/African and younger cohorts per GLOBOCAN.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Keytruda+chemo (KEYNOTE-522) neoadj SOC; Trodelvy 2L+ NCCN Cat 1; Medicare + commercial broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE approved Keytruda neoadj 2022, Trodelvy 2L 2023; G-BA betraechtlich for both.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan PMDA full reimb; China NRDL pembro 2023; EM rely on chemo (AC-T).

us.priceK.note: Blended $140K: Keytruda+chemo $180K (40% pts) + Trodelvy $155K (25%) + chemo alone $25K (30%) + Talzenna/PARPi $180K (5%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$80K blended; EU net ~57% US list; neoadj pembro course-limited lowers cost vs metastatic.

penPct.note: ~55% peak -- IO+chemo neoadj penetrated 70%+ eligible; Trodelvy 2L standard; Datroway (TROP2) + PARPi extend.

us.reachPct.note: Trodelvy 1L mTNBC sNDA (ASCENT-04 positive); PDUFA H2 2026

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 55% -- anticipated commercial payor coverage at launch

eu.reachPct.note: EU launch reach 14% of 65K; EMA filing lagging US typically ~6-12mo

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 40% -- EMA filing planned; post-HTA launch trajectory

eu.priceK.note: EU net $130K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 3% of 120K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at nda_filed

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 10% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $55K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: Bicistronic BCMA×CD3/CD19 CAR-T. BLA under review, PDUFA H2 2026. Competes with CARVYKTI. Yescarta ($1.5B) + Tecartus ($344M) = $1.8B cell therapy franchise in 2025.

us.patientsK.note: ~3K US R/R MM eligible for CAR-T/yr (Carvykti CARTITUDE-4 2L+, Abecma KarMMa-3). Source: CIBMTR, J&J/BMS 10-Ks.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~2K CAR-T infused/yr MM; EBMT registry; capacity-constrained.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~1.5K; Japan + China expanding CAR-T centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~58% -- Carvykti + Abecma 2L+ post-CARTITUDE-4 + KarMMa-3; Medicare transitional pass-through; center-gated.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~42% -- NICE CDF + G-BA Erstattungsbetrag; capacity limits access.

row.wtpPct.note: ~12% -- Japan MHLW; China domestic (equecabtagene) lower priced.

us.priceK.note: Blended $490K: Carvykti $515K (60% pts) + Abecma $455K (30%) + academic CAR-T $380K (10%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$295K blended; EU net ~60% US post negotiation.

penPct.note: ~32% peak -- capacity-gated; earlier-line shift CARTITUDE-5 + off-the-shelf allo CAR-T expand 5yr.

us.reachPct.note: Anito-cel BCMA CAR-T BLA review (Kite/Gilead); competing with Carvykti/Abecma

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 5% of 2K; EMA pathway at pipeline

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 28% -- EU access TBD; EMA pathway unclear at stage

eu.priceK.note: EU net $350K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 1% of 1.5K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at pipeline

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 8% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $180K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

market.notes: Livdelzi (PBC): >50% 2L US market share. HCV/HBV stable. Veklury declining ($911M, -49%). Liver total: $3.2B (+6%). Modest growth offset by Veklury decline.

us.patientsK.note: CDC 2023 + WHO: ~2.4M US chronic HBV (HBsAg+); ~800K on tenofovir/entecavir NUCs, functional cure candidates ~500K.

eu.patientsK.note: ECDC 2023: ~4.7M EU chronic HBV; ~1.5M on NUCs eligible for siRNA/pegIFN combination functional cure regimens.

row.patientsK.note: WHO global: ~296M chronic HBV (Asia-Pac dominant); China 75M, Africa 80M; treatable pool ~50M.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% -- Medicare/commercial cover NUCs; functional cure regimens (GSK bepirovirsen, VIR-2218) will require strict HBsAg/DNA gates.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- EASL guidelines + G-BA/NICE reimburse NUCs; cure combos gated on Ph3 outcomes 2026+.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- China NRDL generic NUCs dominant; novel functional cure access limited near-term.

us.priceK.note: $15K/yr blended anchored to NUC generics ($200) + emerging cure regimens Ph3 (GSK bepirovirsen ASO, Arbutus siRNA pricing TBD $40-60K).

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$8K: NUC generics dominant, functional cure launch 2027+ at ~$30K.

penPct.note: ~4% peak -- functional cure Ph3 gated 2026-2027; NUCs mature + generic.

us.reachPct.note: Livdelzi PBC + HCV portfolio + Veklury COVID remdesivir

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 60% -- established payor adoption + formulary coverage

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 8% of 1500K patients; lower than US due to HTA gating + biosimilar pressure

eu.priceK.note: EU net $45K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 2% of 50000K -- Japan + select markets; limited ex-Japan access

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 15% -- Japan PMDA reimbursed; emerging markets patchy

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $18K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

us.patientsK.note: CDC: ~1.2M US PLHIV (~1.1M diagnosed); ~75% on ART achieving viral suppression; Ryan White program.

eu.patientsK.note: ECDC: ~900K EU-5 PLHIV on ART; integrase inhibitor regimens (Biktarvy dominant); LA Cabenuva Q2M rising.

row.patientsK.note: UNAIDS global ~39M PLHIV; ~30M on ART; ex-US/EU ~25M via WHO/PEPFAR/Global Fund tiered access.

us.wtpPct.note: ~85% -- universal test-and-treat guideline; Medicaid/Ryan White cover; Biktarvy gold standard.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~80% -- universal access via national HIV programs; generic dolutegravir-based regimens widely used.

row.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- PEPFAR/Global Fund dolutegravir-based TLD (tenofovir/lamivudine/DTG) generic access.

us.priceK.note: Biktarvy ~$45K/yr WAC; Cabenuva LA Q2M ~$48K/yr; chronic lifetime dosing.

eu.priceK.note: Biktarvy ~$25K/yr post-HTA; generic DTG-based ~$2K/yr; 60% discount typical vs US.

row.priceK.note: Generic TLD <$0.1K/yr via PEPFAR/tiered pricing; branded LA cabotegravir niche use.

penPct.note: ~40% peak for branded LA/novel agents within treated pool; generics dominate base.

us.reachPct.note: Biktarvy dominant STR in HIV-1 treatment; ~45% US market share

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 75% -- established payor adoption + formulary coverage

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 25% of 900K patients; lower than US due to HTA gating + biosimilar pressure

eu.priceK.note: EU net $25K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 8% of 25000K -- Japan + select markets; limited ex-Japan access

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 18% -- Japan PMDA reimbursed; emerging markets patchy

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $10K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

mega_bear.narrative: Biktarvy erosion + lenacapavir disappoints + oncology fails
events: ✗ Generic KRAS-type erosion in HIV earlier than 2036
✗ Lenacapavir PrEP uptake much slower than modeled
✗ Trodelvy 1L TNBC rejected + anito-cel loses to CARVYKTI
→ Revenue stalls at $28-29B, multiple compresses

bear.narrative: Lenacapavir grows modestly + 1L TNBC approved but competitive + HIV stable
events: ✓ Lenacapavir PrEP growing but 10-year ramp to $3B
~ Trodelvy 1L TNBC approved but competes with Keytruda combos
~ Anito-cel approved but CARVYKTI dominates
→ Revenue grows to $33-35B by 2029, slow grind

base.narrative: Lenacapavir blockbuster + Trodelvy 1L TNBC expands oncology + BIC/LEN launches
events: ✓ Lenacapavir PrEP reaching $5B+ peak — twice-yearly standard
✓ BIC/LEN single tablet launches H2 2026 — extends HIV dominance
✓ Trodelvy 1L TNBC approved — oncology franchise $2B+
✓ 2026 guidance $29.6-30B met — steady 4-5% growth

bull.narrative: HIV + oncology dual engine — $40B+ revenue by 2030, dividend aristocrat
events: ✓ Lenacapavir PrEP standard of care globally — $8B+ peak
✓ Trodelvy franchise $3B+ across breast + other solid tumors
✓ Anito-cel + Yescarta = $3B+ cell therapy franchise
✓ Revenue $40B+ by 2030, 20%+ ROIC, consistent dividend growth

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Lenacapavir ends HIV epidemic + oncology franchise rivals Merck — $50B+ revenue
events: ✓ Lenacapavir eliminates HIV as public health threat — $12B+ peak
✓ Trodelvy + next-gen ADCs create $5B+ oncology franchise
✓ Cell therapy next-gen (bicistronic) dominates post-CARVYKTI era
✓ Revenue $50B+ by 2030, Gilead re-rates from 12x to 18x PE

lastData.title: $29.4B 2025 revenue, $8.15 non-GAAP EPS, 2026 guide $29.6-30B
points: Lenacapavir/Yeztugo: $150M first year — twice-yearly HIV prevention
Tubulis acquisition ($5B) to bolster oncology ADC pipeline

nextCatalyst.title: Trodelvy 1L TNBC PDUFA + anito-cel PDUFA + BIC/LEN launch
thresholds: Anito-cel MM CAR-T FDA decision H2 2026
BIC/LEN single-tablet HIV regimen launch H2 2026

risks: $172B market cap at ~12x 2025 earnings — fair value, not cheap
Lenacapavir PrEP uptake depends on access/reimbursement globally
Oncology franchise faces intense competition (ADC space crowding)
Cell therapy declining (-7%) — bispecifics disrupting CAR-T
Veklury declining rapidly — $911M hole to fill
Dividend yield 3.2% attractive but limits capital allocation flexibility
